title: Tennis Star's Stress and a Doping Test Problem
summary: Marketa Vondrousova is a tennis player who won Wimbledon in 2023. In December 2025, she refused to let a doping control officer into her home for a test. She says she was very stressed and scared because the officer came late at night without following proper rules. The International Tennis Integrity Agency (ITIA) has charged her with refusing the test, which could lead to a ban of up to four years if she is found guilty. Vondrousova explains that she was not trying to avoid the test but felt unsafe. She has been dealing with anxiety and stress, and experts have confirmed her condition. She can still play tennis while the investigation continues, but she has not played recently due to a shoulder injury. This story shows how mental health can affect athletes in sports.

background_read: This article is about a tennis player named Marketa Vondrousova who is facing a problem with a doping test. Doping tests are important in sports to make sure athletes play fairly without using drugs. Vondrousova says she refused the test because she was very stressed and scared when an officer came to her home late at night. She has won big tournaments like Wimbledon, but now she might get banned from playing if she is found guilty. The story also talks about mental health, which is how people feel in their minds, and how it can affect athletes. It shows that sometimes, people make decisions based on their feelings, like fear, even if they don't mean to break rules.
Article_Structure: WHO: Marketa Vondrousova, a tennis player who won Wimbledon in 2023
WHAT: She refused a doping test at her home in December 2025 due to stress and fear
WHERE: At her home, and the incident involves the International Tennis Integrity Agency
WHY: Because she felt unsafe and was dealing with mental health issues like anxiety

perspectives.description: She believes she refused the test out of fear and stress, not to avoid it, and wants to be transparent about her mental health.
